big_picture: India has not held bilateral talks with the US on naval deployment in the Strait of Hormuz, the Ministry of External Affairs stated in response to President Donald Trump’s call for countries to deploy warships to keep the waterway ‘open and safe’. The Indian Navy is escorting India-bound oil tankers as Iran chokes the Strait of Hormuz, with the second LPG tanker Nanda Devi set to arrive at Kandla Port today carrying 46,000 metric tonnes of LPG amid a worldwide crisis caused by the conflict in West Asia. In political developments, BJP’s Sanjay Bhatia and Congress’ Karamvir Boudh won two remaining Rajya Sabha seats. Additionally, Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas advised the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas on drafting the Petroleum and Natural Gas Rules, 2025.

Legal.summary:
The Bombay High Court quashed an order cancelling a Christian prayer event due to 'miracle healing' claims, and ordered compliance with the Black Magic Act. The court's decision was made in favor of the Glory Ministry Trust.

The Supreme Court refused to interfere with the Karnataka High Court's ruling allowing Kambala events to take place across the state, not just in Dakshina Kannada.

The High Court ruled that retrospective cancellation of GST registration is invalid if such action is not proposed in the original show cause notice.
Legal.top_article_title: Analysis of Notifications and Circulars for Week Ending 15th March 2026

Energy.summary: India's Ministry of External Affairs stated that the country has not held bilateral talks with the US regarding naval deployment in the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, the Indian Navy is escorting oil tankers bound for India as Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz disrupts oil supplies.
Energy.top_article_title: India has not held bilateral talks with US on naval deployment in Strait of Hormuz: MEA

Foreign.summary:
India is facing a diplomatic challenge as it navigates its relationships with Iran and the US, which could impact the country's economy, particularly in the BRICS forum it is chairing this year.

India's dependence on oil from the US and Israel is being exposed due to the ongoing conflict with Iran, leading to disrupted supplies, price hikes, and potential emergency measures for oil marketing companies.

The Supreme Court and High Courts in India have interpreted the NDPS Act in 2025, focusing on bail, detention, evidence, and procedural safeguards in a series of judgments.
Foreign.top_article_title: How India’s tough spot between Iran and US can dent the economy

Finance.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) held a 7-day Variable Rate Repo auction on March 17, 2026, allotting the full notified amount of ₹1,50,000 crore at a cut-off rate. The RBI also released its Money Market Operations as of March 16, 2026, showing the weighted average rate range for various segments.

Himayani Puri, daughter of Hardeep Puri, has filed a lawsuit in the Delhi High Court seeking ₹10 crore in damages and an order to remove content linking her to Jeffrey Epstein.
Finance.top_article_title: Result of the 7-day Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auction held on March 17, 2026

Politics.summary:
The BJP gained an upper hand in Odisha Rajya Sabha polls after Dilip Ray's victory over a BJD-backed nominee, signaling a shift in the state's political equations. The BJP also secured two remaining seats in the Rajya Sabha polls, with Sanjay Bhatia and Karamvir Boudh winning, after the Election Commission invalidated a Congress MLA's vote due to a secrecy violation.

The Election Commission of India has announced the election schedule for Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal, with polls set to take place in April and results to be declared on May 4.
Politics.top_article_title: BJP Gains Upper Hand in Odisha Rajya Sabha Polls Amid Cross-Voting Drama

Civil Liberties.summary:
Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has reprimanded his cabinet ministers for their delay in responding to questions from MLAs. 

The Supreme Court has declined to intervene in the Calcutta High Court's order for a National Investigation Agency (NIA) probe into the Beldanga violence. 

The Supreme Court has directed AIIMS-Delhi to ensure a tailored plan for withdrawing life support from a patient, as per a landmark judgment.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: CM Siddaramaiah pulls up ministers over delay in replies to MLAs’ questions

Trade.summary: India's trade deal with the US is contingent on the US re-establishing global tariff rates, according to Commerce Secretary Rajesh Agrawal. The deal's signing is also dependent on a new tariff framework being finalized, which includes discussions on non-tariff barriers and Section 232 tariffs.
Trade.top_article_title: India-US trade deal to be signed when Trump re-establishes tariff rates, Trade Secy says

Technology.summary:
Banks in India are facing challenges with Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance, causing strain on both bank staff and account holders. 

PhonePe has delayed its planned $1.3-billion IPO due to unstable market conditions and disagreements over its valuation with investors. 

India's push in AI and semiconductor sectors is being hindered by unrealistic budgets, delayed fund releases, and lack of convincing the finance ministry for necessary funds, according to a parliamentary panel.
Technology.top_article_title: KYC compliance loop continues to strain bank staff & account holders

Agriculture.summary:
The GSP Crop Science IPO has received a 61% subscription on its second day, following a 47% subscription on the first day, with shares priced at ₹304-320 and aiming to raise ₹400 crore. 

The Himachal Pradesh government has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the National Dairy Development Board to boost the state's dairy sector. 

Jammu and Kashmir has seen a boom in trout farming, driven by investment and policy support, with the state receiving ₹11,850.39 lakh between 2020-21 and 2024-25.
Agriculture.top_article_title: GSP Crop Science IPO Day 2: Issue booked 61% so far. Check GMP, review, key details. Apply or avoid?

Health.summary:
A fire broke out at a government hospital in Thiruvananthapuram, Kerala, raising concerns about the safety and emergency preparedness of the state-run healthcare facility. 

Eli Lilly is warning against compounded tirzepatide mixed with vitamin B12 due to concerns about its safety and efficacy, as tirzepatide is the active ingredient in Lilly's popular anti-obesity and diabetes drugs, Mounjaro and Zepbound. 

The Supreme Court of India has allowed passive euthanasia in the case of Harish Rana, which may shift the treatment goals in critical care and palliative medicine from prolonging life to reducing suffering when recovery is no longer possible.
Health.top_article_title: Fire breaks out at Thiruvananthapuram govt hospital

Welfare.summary: The Supreme Court of India has allowed the country's first passive euthanasia case, granting permission to withdraw life support, and clarified the "best interest test" for such cases. Meanwhile, a BJP leader, Satish Gautam, has sparked controversy by claiming that Muslims are taking the most advantage of welfare schemes in India.
Welfare.top_article_title: Congress sees BJP leader’s bid to trigger polarisation

Governance.summary:
In modern India, pension planning is becoming increasingly necessary due to the risk of growing old without financial security. 

Puri's daughter has filed a petition in the Delhi High Court against posts linking her father to Jeffrey Epstein. 

An India-flagged tanker has arrived in India after a 9-day voyage from Qatar, with two more tankers expected to arrive today.
Governance.top_article_title: Why pension planning can no longer be optional

Defence.summary:
A Samajwadi Party leader has been booked for allegedly urging Muslims not to celebrate Eid as a protest against the West Asia conflict, claiming Azam Khan made similar remarks. 

An Indian warship is escorting an Indian-flagged oil tanker, Jag Laadki, out of the Gulf of Oman to the country's west coast.
Defence.top_article_title: 11:11 | Iran war remains in focus, Heavy rains in North India, GSTN advisory, Maharashtra Religion bill, and more

Infrastructure.summary:
The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has attached ₹23.35 crore assets in a Raipur-Visakhapatnam highway compensation scam case, based on an FIR registered by the Chhattisgarh Economic Offences Investigation Bureau/Anti-Corruption Bureau. 

The National Democratic Alliance (NDA) has gained in Rajya Sabha polls, with cross-voting and no-show MLAs affecting the Opposition's chances.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: ED attaches ₹23.35 crore assets in Raipur–Visakhapatnam highway compensation scam case

Environment.summary: India is waking up to the potential of coffee as a tool to combat climate change. Efforts are being made to protect and revive India's rivers, including the Buddha nala pollution issue, on the International Day of Action for Rivers 2026.
Environment.top_article_title: How India’s waking up to coffees that can beat climate change

Education.summary:
Anna University has signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Ramco Systems to establish an advanced Enterprise Resource Planning laboratory. 

Larsen & Toubro has partnered with IIT-Kharagpur to design training programs focused on artificial intelligence and related technologies for its workforce.
Education.top_article_title: Yoga over Python: how India’s new college curriculum rewards the easiest skills

Labour.summary:
Infosys is expanding its Mohali Development Center, which will have a built-up area of about 3.5 lakh sq ft and seat around 3,000 employees. 

The Centre of Indian Trade Unions (CITU) has criticized the central government's decision to privatize the Visakhapatnam Steel Plant. 

Punjab's Economic Survey 2025-26 identifies economic stagnation and a mismatch between job opportunities and workforce skills as key reasons for distress migration from the state.

Urban.summary: Hard Rock International terminated franchise agreements with JSM Corporation Pvt Ltd, resulting in the closure of Hard Rock Cafes and Rock Shops in 10 locations across India.
Urban.top_article_title: Hard Rock Cafe terminates agreement with JSM Corp, shuts outlets across 10 locations in India

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that charitable trusts in India do not need to file Form 9A when setting off excess income from previous years against current-year income. The ruling clarifies that such set-offs are permissible under Section 11 of the Income Tax Act as a valid application of income. The decision provides clarity on compliance requirements for trusts managing accumulated funds.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal ruled that filing Form 9A is not required when a charitable trust sets off earlier years’ excess application against current year income. Supreme Court| Confirmed auction sale open to Judicial Scrutiny; Revaluation permissible to ensure fair price fetched in recovery proceedings
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that confirmed auction sales in recovery proceedings are subject to judicial scrutiny to ensure fairness and transparency. The Court held that revaluation of assets is permissible to guarantee a fair price is fetched during recovery processes. This decision impacts how courts and authorities handle auction sales in debt recovery cases, emphasizing the balance between finality of sales and the need for fair valuation.
articles.raw_summary: The requirement that the recovery process be fair, transparent and based on a proper assessment of value must co-exist with the principle of finality governing confirmed sales. articles.title: Can a Public Servant Escape Bribery Conviction Because the Co-Accused Was Acquitted? Supreme Court Clarifies Law
articles.summary: The Supreme Court clarified that a public servant convicted of bribery cannot automatically escape punishment just because a co-accused was acquitted. The ruling emphasizes that the failure to prove conspiracy against one accused does not invalidate the conviction of another in bribery cases. This decision reinforces the legal framework around corruption prosecutions in India.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court explains that failure to prove conspiracy against a co-accused does not entitle a convicted public servant to acquittal in bribery cases.

The prolonged pre-trial stage raises questions about the enforcement of national security laws and their impact on free speech and digital communication.

articles.title: Air Force Group Insurance Society Is 'State' Under Article 12 Of Constitution  : Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has held Air Force Group Insurance Society to be a 'State' under Article 12 of the Constitution, amenable to Writ Jurisdiction. A bench comprising Justices Sanjay Karol and Vipul M Pancholi overturned the Delhi High Court's decision refusing to consider Air Force Group Insurance Society (“Society”) as 'State'. The bench held that since the Society performs a public function closely linked to the State's obligations toward members of the Indian Air Force, it qualifies as a State

articles.title: Trade deficit nearly 2x in Feb; exports face battlefield ahead
articles.summary: India's trade deficit widened significantly in February, reaching $27 billion. This increase was driven by a substantial rise in imports, especially gold and silver. The trade gap with China also saw an increase during the fiscal year.

articles.summary: As per the draft guidelines, a bona fide victim, being an individual person and having lodged a complaint involving gross loss of an amount up to Rs 50,000 on account of fraudulent electronic banking transaction (s), would be compensated 85% of the net loss amount (calculated after reducing recoveries made, whether before or after paying the compensation, from the gross loss amount), or Rs 25,000, whichever is less, once during his or her lifetime.

articles.title: DGFT Restricts Import of Certain Studded Silver Jewellery
articles.summary: The government has restricted the import of specific studded silver jewellery items by changing their import policy from 'Free' to 'Restricted'. This decision requires importers to obtain prior authorization from the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) before bringing these products into India. The move aims to regulate the inflow of such jewellery, potentially impacting trade flows and compliance requirements for businesses involved in silver jewellery imports.

articles.title: Abki baar, ‘sources’ sarkar
articles.summary: The article argues that the Indian government's reliance on anonymous sources to convey official positions undermines accountability. It highlights how this practice allows ministers to avoid direct responsibility, which contradicts the principles of transparent journalism.
articles.raw_summary: Using sources to communicate a government’s official view allows ministers to evade accountability – the very opposite of what journalism is meant to achieve.

articles.title: When Wang Yi Speaks, It’s Not Just China’s Foreign Minister Talking
articles.summary: China's Foreign Minister Wang Yi's consolidation of roles has made the Two Sessions press conference the most authoritative source of Beijing's foreign policy signals. This development could influence India's diplomatic strategies, as China's foreign policy decisions often impact regional dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: Wang’s unprecedented consolidation of roles has made the Two Sessions press conference the most authoritative foreign policy signal Beijing produces. It has also made it the most rigid.

articles.title: Government Notifies Amendments to Captive Power Project Framework

articles.title: Can women enter Sabarimala? Kerala says neither yes nor no, tells SC to ask scholars, weigh equality
articles.summary: The Kerala government has asked the Supreme Court to consult scholars on whether women can enter Sabarimala, avoiding a direct yes or no answer. This follows review petitions challenging the court's earlier judgment on the issue, which raises questions about gender equality and religious rights.
articles.raw_summary: The debate has resurfaced due to a batch of review petitions and constitutional questions arising from the Sabarimala judgment that are currently before the Supreme Court.

articles.title: 24 hearings, 5 months, a pending verdict—and then Centre dropped NSA against Sonam Wangchuk
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has held multiple hearings over five months on a habeas corpus petition filed by Sonam Wangchuk's wife, challenging the Centre's NSA detention. The case has seen repeated adjournments at the Centre's request, raising questions about due process and civil liberties.
articles.raw_summary: The habeas corpus petition filed by Wangchuk’s wife was listed about 24 times before the Supreme Court over the past five months, with many hearings lately ending in adjournments at the Centre’s behest.

articles.title: Modi-govt walks fine line between Iran and US as war hits economy
articles.summary: India is intensifying diplomatic engagement with Iran amidst severe gas shortages and economic threats due to Middle East conflict. New Delhi secured safe passage for LPG tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, balancing its strategic ties with the US and Iran. This move follows recent high-level talks aimed at hedging its position.

articles.title: SC closes criminal case against Ashoka University professor
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India has closed criminal proceedings against Ashoka University professor Ali Khan Mahmudabad regarding his social media posts. The case involved legal scrutiny of his online statements. The ruling highlights the intersection of free speech and legal accountability in India. No further action will be taken in the matter.

articles.title: Kandla port ramp ups capacity expecting 22 vessels out of troubled Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Kandla port, a state-run facility on India's west coast, is preparing for a possible surge in incoming vessels—up to 22 ships—due to disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz. The port is increasing its capacity to handle the expected rise in maritime traffic. This development could impact India's trade flows and energy imports, particularly crude oil, which often transits through the region.
articles.raw_summary: The state-run Kandla port on the west coast is planning for a possible surge in inbound maritime traffic— up to 22 vessels in the next few days

articles.title: Reliance, Samsung sign $3-billion contract for green ammonia supply
articles.summary: Reliance Industries and Samsung have signed a $3-billion deal to supply green ammonia, marking one of the largest agreements in this sector globally. The agreement aligns with Reliance's goal to strengthen its position in the renewable energy value chain. The deal could influence India's energy transition and trade dynamics, though it is a private sector initiative without direct government involvement.
articles.raw_summary: The Reliance-Samsung deal, one of the largest of its kind globally, signals Chairman Mukesh Ambani's ambition to dominate the renewable energy value chain.

articles.title: ‘Not our war’: How US allies gave a cold shoulder to Trump's appeal to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The US sought support from allies to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions, but key partners like the UK and France declined or hesitated. The move was aimed at countering threats to oil shipping routes, which are critical for global energy supplies. The refusal highlights divisions among Western allies on military interventions in the Middle East. India, a major oil importer, may monitor these developments for potential impacts on its energy security and diplomatic stance.
articles.raw_summary: Despite the pressure, several key US allies have either rejected or hesitated to join the effort.

articles.title: Pak-Afghanistan 'war' LIVE: Pak strike that killed 400 in Kabul eclipsed 'Zionist regime' crimes, says Afghanistan
articles.summary: Pakistan conducted airstrikes in Kabul, Afghanistan, which Afghanistan claims destroyed a drug treatment hospital and killed 400 people. Pakistan denied the allegations, calling them false and misleading. The incident has escalated tensions between the two countries, with Afghanistan accusing Pakistan of disproportionate force. The situation may impact regional stability and India's foreign policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistan-Afghanistan 'war' live updates: Afghanistan Health Ministry spokesman Sharafat Zaman said all parts of the drug treatment hospital had been destroyed. Pakistan on Tuesday rejected Afghanistan’s allegation that its airstrikes hit a hospital in Kabul, calling the claim “false and misleading".

articles.summary: The National Medical Commission has fined seven medical colleges ₹1 crore each for failing to disclose stipend details for interns and residents. Support measures for exporters on anvil to deal with West Asia crisis

The government is actively monitoring the situation and collaborating with various departments to aid the trading community.

articles.title: Memory chip shortage to continue until 2030, says SK Group chairman
articles.summary: SK Group chairman Chey Tae-won predicts a global shortage of memory chips will persist for the next 4-5 years, with supply unable to meet demand until at least 2030. The shortage is driven by dominant players like SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron struggling to keep up with demand. This could impact industries reliant on semiconductors, including India's growing electronics and tech sectors. The situation may prompt policy measures to address supply chain vulnerabilities or boost domestic production
articles.raw_summary: Chey Tae-won, SK Group chairman, predicts a global memory chip shortage for 4-5 more years. SK Hynix, Samsung, and Micron control supply but can't meet demand until 2030.

articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 246 |  THE STATE OF HIMACHAL PRADESH VERSUS SURAT SINGH
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that giving an accused the option to be searched before a police officer violates Section 50 of the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act. The case involved the State of Himachal Pradesh versus Surat Singh. This ruling impacts how police conduct searches under the NDPS Act, which deals with drug-related offenses. The decision underscores strict procedural compliance in drug law enforcement.

articles.title: 2026 LiveLaw (SC) 245  |  THE MANAGING DIRECTOR, KSRTC VERSUS P. CHANDRAMOULI & ORS.
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that employer-provided group insurance benefits cannot be deducted from compensation awarded in motor accident claims. The case involved the Managing Director of KSRTC (Kerala State Road Transport Corporation) and P. Chandramouli & others. The ruling clarifies legal standards for compensation in motor accident cases, ensuring victims receive full benefits without offsetting employer insurance payouts.

articles.title: Phantom Precedents: The Rise Of AI-Generated Case Law In Indian Courts
articles.summary: The legitimacy of common law adjudication depends on the authenticity of precedent. If fabricated authorities enter judicial reasoning, the integrity of the doctrine of stare decisis itself is compromised. Recently, however, a disruptive and highly concerning trend has emerged across global jurisdictions, the submission of phantom case law. Judicial officers reviewing written submissions are increasingly discovering perfectly formatted citations for judgments that simply do not exist. The culpri

articles.title: Boney Kapoor And Daughters Move Madras High Court To Reject Plaint Claiming Sridevi's Property
articles.summary: Film Producer Boney Kapoor and his daughters, Janhvi and Kushi Kapoor, have approached the Madras High Court against an order of the Additional District Judge, Chengalpattu, refusing to reject a plaint filed in connection with the late actor Sridevi's property near the East Coast Road. When the plea came up before Justice TV Thamilselvi on Monday (16 March), the court decided to take up the matter for final disposal on March 26, 2026 and extended the interim order staying the trial in the case.

articles.title: The Right To Die, With Dignity
articles.summary: Harish Rana, a 20-year-old engineering student, suffered critical injuries in a fall in 2013, leaving him bedridden and dependent on life support without his knowledge. The incident raises questions about end-of-life care, patient autonomy, and the right to die with dignity. Such cases have prompted legal and ethical debates in India regarding living wills and euthanasia, though no specific policy action is reported here.
articles.raw_summary: Harish Rana, like any other 20-year-old, was once a wide-eyed youth with hopes, dreams, and a determination to make it big. He embarked on this journey by pursuing an engineering degree. Unfortunately, fate – the inexplicable wind that charters one's life, had different plans. One evening in 2013, Harish fell from the fourth floor of his PG accommodation and sustained critical injuries. articles.summary: The Jammu & Kashmir and Ladakh High Court has held that a District Judge hearing a reference under the Land Acquisition Act functions as a Civil Court and passes judicial orders. Consequently, such orders cannot be challenged through a writ petition under Article 226 but can only be examined under the Court's supervisory jurisdiction, the court explained. articles.title: LPG Amendment Order 2026: PNG Users Barred from Domestic LPG Connections
articles.summary: The LPG Amendment Order 2026, issued by government oil companies, bars PNG consumers from retaining or obtaining domestic LPG connections. Affected users must surrender their connections, and dual connections are prohibited. This policy impacts households using PNG for cooking gas and reflects a regulatory shift in India's LPG distribution framework.

articles.title: SAFEMA Tribunal Reduces FEMA Penalty For Unauthorized Handling of Foreign Currency
articles.summary: The SAFEMA Tribunal ruled that lack of familiarity with foreign exchange rules cannot excuse violations of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA). However, it reduced a penalty imposed for unauthorized handling of foreign currency, considering the circumstances of the case. The tribunal's decision clarifies legal expectations for compliance with FEMA while demonstrating judicial discretion in penalty imposition.

articles.title: Bank-NBFC co-lending pacts may be expanded to aid MSMEs
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has recommended expanding co-lending partnerships between banks and non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) to help micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSMEs) access cheaper credit. articles.raw_summary: A parliamentary panel suggests boosting bank and NBFC co-lending to help MSMEs access cheaper credit. articles.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
articles.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

articles.title: Govt imposes import curbs on silver jewellery studded with diamonds
articles.summary: The Indian government has imposed import restrictions on silver jewellery studded with inexpensive diamonds, effective immediately until June 30, 2026. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade announced the move, shifting from unrestricted to restricted imports to curb silver imports from ASEAN nations. The policy aims to regulate imports of such jewellery.

This policy revision, moving from free to restricted, aims to curb silver imports from ASEAN nations. The Directorate General of Foreign Trade announced the immediate implementation of these curbs.

articles.title: Fuel shortages from Middle East war begin to threaten global food supply

Australian grain growers face fuel delivery cutbacks, Bangladeshi rice farmers struggle to power irrigation pumps, and Filipino fishermen may halt operations. A prolonged supply crunch could drive up food prices worldwide.

A prolonged supply crunch will drive up food bills.

articles.title: Supreme Court says paid menstrual leave will end women’s careers, calls PIL a bid to ‘instil fear’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court dismissed a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) seeking paid menstrual leave, stating that such policies could negatively impact women's careers. The court said the PIL aimed to 'instil fear' by suggesting women are not on par with men during menstruation. The ruling has implications for workplace policies and gender equality in India.
articles.raw_summary: Dismissing PIL, SC said: 'This is only to create impression in young women that you have some natural issues, and are not at par with males, and you cannot work like them during a particular time.'

articles.title: SC sets record straight for OBC quota—social status, not paycheck must be measure for ‘creamy layer’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that social status, not just parental income, should determine 'creamy layer' status for OBC reservations. This decision impacts students who were denied benefits based solely on their parents' salaries. The ruling clarifies eligibility for government-backed educational opportunities under affirmative action policies.
articles.raw_summary: Students involved in legal challenges were successful Civil Services Examination candidates whose OBC (non-creamy layer) status was rejected based on parents’ salaries.

articles.raw_summary: Harish Rana’s case revealed a fundamental truth about human existence: ‘The greatest tragedy in life is not death, but abandonment’, the top court said in its landmark judgement.

articles.title: What led to PhonePe delaying its IPO? Beyond war, valuation a major concern
articles.summary: PhonePe, a Walmart-owned digital payments firm valued at $15 billion, has delayed its planned $1.3 billion IPO due to volatile markets, valuation concerns, and geopolitical tensions. Investor interest was lower than expected at around $7 billion. The delay is also attributed to competition with Paytm and regulatory challenges that impact profitability and listing timing.
articles.raw_summary: PhonePe IPO delayed: PhonePe has postponed its $1.3 billion IPO due to volatile markets, valuation concerns and geopolitical tensions. The Walmart-owned firm, valued at nearly $15 billion, saw investor interest closer to $7 billion. Competition with Paytm and regulatory headwinds also affected its profitability outlook and listing timing.

articles.title: PhonePe hits pause on IPO as Iran war roils primary market sentiment
articles.summary: Digital payments giant PhonePe has postponed its planned public listing in India. Citing global geopolitical conflicts and market instability, CEO Sameer Nigam expressed hope for a swift return to peace. The company, boasting over 65 crore users and a vast merchant network, remains committed to an Indian IPO once markets stabilize.

articles.title: Ola Electric launches #EndICEAge campaign to accelerate EV adoption
articles.summary: Ola Electric has launched the #EndICEAge campaign to promote electric vehicle (EV) adoption in India. The campaign emphasizes the rising costs and inconveniences of petrol dependency, urging riders to switch to EVs. This aligns with India's broader policy push for sustainable transportation and reducing fossil fuel reliance.
articles.raw_summary: The campaign highlights the rising cost and inconvenience of petrol dependency while encouraging riders across the country to make the shift to electric vehicles.

articles.title: Meta ends end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs: what this means
articles.summary: Meta has ended optional end-to-end encryption for Instagram DMs, reverting chats to unencrypted mode after May 8. This gives Meta access to user content for scanning and moderation, prioritizing safety over privacy. The move follows lawsuits over platform harms and may impact discussions on digital rights and surveillance in India.
articles.raw_summary: Instagram had introduced the optional end-to-end encryption in December 2023. Users had to enable it manually per chat, unlike the default encryption available on WhatsApp or Messenger. After May 8, these chats will revert to the unencrypted mode, giving Meta access to content for scanning and moderation. The move follows Instagram prioritising safety over secrecy, amid lawsuits over platform harms.

articles.title: Indian-origin AI researcher Devendra Chaplot joins Elon Musk's xAI
articles.summary: In the new role, Chaplot will work closely with Musk and the teams at both companies to build superintelligence. Earlier, Chaplot worked in academia, Big Tech, research, and AI startups, with a focus on robotics, embodied AI and LLMs. Part of the founding team at French tech company Mistral AI, Chaplot has contributed to models such as Mistral 7B and Mixtral. He has also worked at Thinking Machines Lab, which works on large-scale training infrastructure and tools for foundation models.

He has also worked at Thinking Machines Lab, which works on large-scale training infrastructure and tools for foundation models.

articles.title: Revamped Stand-Up India scheme soon: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Replying to a supplementary question in the Lok Sabha, Sitharaman said the modified programme would incorporate lessons from the earlier scheme, which was discontinued in April 2025 after nine years. "With studies done by Niti Aayog and others, this scheme is being redrafted and soon the Cabinet will be given a proposal with which we will launch a new scheme to continue with Stand-Up India, which benefitted SC/ST and women all over the country," she said.

articles.title: India’s horticulture production to increase marginally in 2025-26: First Advance Estimate
articles.summary: India's horticulture production is set to remain steady in 2025-26. Vegetable output is expected to rise, driven by onions and potatoes. Fruit production will see a slight increase. Flower production may see a minor dip. Spice output is projected to grow significantly, with gains in cumin, ginger, garlic, and turmeric. This sector is crucial for boosting farmer incomes.

These mills are required to complete the exports by June 30, 2026, with those meeting a threshold of 70 percent allowed to ship the remaining sugar by September 30, 2026.

articles.title: Shipping wars stitch higher costs into India’s apparel exports
articles.summary: India's apparel exporters are facing increased costs due to the West Asian conflict, with shipping routes extended and war surcharges adding ₹12 to ₹55 per garment. This move aims to proactively address potential competition issues in the rapidly evolving AI space in India.

articles.title: LTM ties up with IIT-Kharagpur to upskill workforce in artificial intelligence
articles.summary: Larsen & Toubro (LTM) has partnered with IIT-Kharagpur to design training programs focused on artificial intelligence and related technologies for its employees. The initiative aims to upskill and reskill the workforce to meet evolving industry demands in an AI-driven landscape through learning programs, workshops, and research. The collaboration seeks to build future-ready AI capabilities within the company.

This ensures the workforce stays prepared for an AI-driven landscape.

articles.title: Agnit Semiconductors raises $2.6 million from Shastra VC, 3one4 Capital and Zephyr Peacock
articles.summary: The company has raised $7.47 million to date and will use the new funds to scale production to about 100,000 GaN components over the next two years as it moves from pilot deployments to commercialization. The round also marks Shastra VC’s first investment in India’s semiconductor ecosystem.

articles.title: Climate change could drive millions globally into physical inactivity by 2050: Study
articles.summary: A study published in The Lancet warns that climate change could force millions of people globally to become physically inactive by 2050. This inactivity may lead to 700,000 additional deaths annually and $3.68 billion in lost productivity. The findings highlight potential public health and economic challenges that could require policy interventions.
articles.raw_summary: Climate change could cause millions to become inactive by 2050, leading to 700,000 extra deaths yearly and $3.68 billion in productivity losses, says Lancet study.

articles.title: 36 years after ₹500 bribe, Supreme Court reduces 75-year-old ex-constable's jail term
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the conviction of a 75-year-old former Uttarakhand excise constable for a bribery case from 36 years ago but reduced his jail term. The case highlights the prolonged legal process and judicial discretion in sentencing for petty corruption. The ruling underscores the judiciary's role in reviewing and adjusting penalties for long-standing cases.
articles.raw_summary: Over threeandahalf decades after a petty bribery case began, the Supreme Court has upheld the conviction of a former Uttarakhand excise constable but reduced his sentence.

articles.title: Microfinance portfolio falls over 7% in Q3 FY26, but asset quality signals recovery: MFIN

articles.raw_summary: MFIN reported a 7.3% decline in the microfinance sector’s loan portfolio in Q3 2025, totaling ₹3.15 lakh crore. NBFC-MFIs held 42.1%. Asset quality improved despite funding issues.

articles.title: Cuba's national electric grid collapses, leaving millions without power
articles.summary: Cuba's national electric grid collapsed, leaving 10 million people without power. The crisis is exacerbated by a US oil blockade and the cutoff of Venezuelan oil to Cuba under Trump's administration. The UN's energy agency (UNE) is investigating the cause. The situation highlights vulnerabilities in energy supply chains that could have broader geopolitical and trade implications.
articles.raw_summary: Cuba's electric grid collapsed, leaving 10 million without power amid a US oil blockade. UNE is investigating. Trump cut off Venezuelan oil to Cuba, worsening the crisis.

articles.title: Iran war, oil and sticky inflation: Factors that may put Fed’s rate cut plans on hold
articles.summary: The US Federal Reserve is expected to maintain interest rates at 3.5%-3.75% during its March 17-18 meeting due to rising inflation, labor market concerns, and geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the US. These factors could delay planned rate cuts, impacting global financial markets. The decision may influence India's economic policies, particularly in trade and energy sectors, given its reliance on oil imports and trade relations with the US.
articles.raw_summary: The Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates at 3.5%-3.75% during its March 17-18 meeting amid the Iran-US conflict, rising inflation, and labor market concerns.

articles.title: US-Iran war puts Gulf countries on the brink of their worst performance in decades
articles.summary: A potential war involving the US, Iran, and Israel could severely disrupt Gulf economies, particularly Qatar and Kuwait, with GDP drops of up to 14%. This conflict may impact global oil supplies and energy markets, which are critical for India's energy security and economic stability. The situation could influence India's foreign policy and trade relations with the Gulf region.

articles.summary: NMDC, a state-owned mining company, has become the first in India to produce 50 million tonnes of iron ore in a single financial year. This achievement highlights the company's operational scale and could influence future mining policies, though the article does not detail any specific government actions or policy changes.

articles.title: Reliance signs $3 billion green ammonia supply deal with Samsung C&T
articles.summary: Reliance Industries has signed a $3 billion, 15-year agreement with Samsung C&T to supply green ammonia, one of the largest clean fuel offtake deals globally. The partnership underscores India's growing role in the green energy sector and could impact national policies on clean energy adoption and trade. The deal involves long-term supply commitments, signaling market confidence in India's green hydrogen/ammonia ecosystem.

articles.title: GMDC, NMDC sign pact to explore rare earth development in Gujarat
articles.summary: Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation (GMDC) and National Mineral Development Corporation (NMDC) have signed an agreement to assess the potential for developing a rare earth value chain in Gujarat. The partnership will explore activities ranging from exploration and mining to processing and downstream applications. Rare earth elements are critical for various industries, including technology and energy, making this initiative strategically important for India's industrial and policy landscape.
articles.raw_summary: The partnership will focus on assessing the potential development of an integrated rare earth value chain in Gujarat, covering exploration, mining, beneficiation, processing and downstream applications.

articles.title: 47% of sitting MLAs in poll-bound West Bengal face criminal cases: ADR
articles.summary: A report by the Association for Democratic Reforms (ADR) reveals that 47% of sitting MLAs in West Bengal face criminal cases, with 37% facing serious charges. The findings come ahead of the state's elections on April 23 and 29, with results declared on May 4. The report also notes that 52% of MLAs are crorepatis and 15% are women. The data raises questions about governance and electoral integrity in the state.
articles.raw_summary: Nearly half of West Bengal's legislators have criminal cases, says ADR. Elections are on April 23 and 29, results on May 4. 47% face charges, 37% serious. 52% are crorepatis, 15% women.
